lines.text: We never stopped asking the Order for permission to wear the Terminals, but every single request is denied. You won't even tell us why!
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: You've controlled the Terminal supply, and the family stopped trading with us... These are all we have left. Why should we hand them over?
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: We paid good money for these. If you're taking them, you owe us a refund.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: Ernesto, the bartender, said you needed to see me.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: You may have just defended us, but you heard it—your family has left us in a mess. Frankly, I don't see the point in having this conversation.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: But since Ernesto said it's about Benett, I'll give you a minute, for his sake. Speak quickly.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: Regardless of the bigger picture, your side ended the deal. We were informed on short notice to stop all trade.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: And it's not just the Terminals—no more "jewelry," "perfumes," and all that nonsense. You saw what happened today.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: Not to mention I haven't seen a Montelli for nearly two weeks.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: Where were you when we were fighting against the Order and the Fisalias?
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: With your grandfather, maybe, but he won't be around forever. When Capollo takes the helm... you won't have a say.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: The real question is whether we can get our trade back on track. Can your family still be trusted? What I need is a solid promise from someone in charge—can you do that?
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: One thing's for sure, I don't want to see the family who first broke the monopoly to trade with us commoners end up defending it.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: You know what? This is supposed to be your family's business. Why am I saying this to you... Excuse my earlier words.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: So... Ernesto told me Benett's gone. What happened? He seemed fine the last time we met.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: Good judgment there! The rumors are nonsense… Fencing stolen goods? Please. Benett used to handle our shipments for the vault, but everything was above board.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: Nothing shady, okay? He knew his stuff—never took advantage of us, never budged on prices either.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: For him, it was always business first, friendship second. When he was running late, he'd stay up that <color=yellow>hill</color> sometimes. We even set up a shelter there after it happened a few times. That's all.
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: Come to think of it, he did stay there for a while the last time we saw him. Asked us to stash something too. What was it he said… "Hope we don't need to use it."
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family

lines.text: There's <color=yellow>an entrance along the coast</color>. Go straight down the road and you will see it. What's your business there, anyway?
lines.quest_name: If On a Rainy Night a Family
